Container.Name: Gerbera
Container.Overview: Gerbera is a lightweight UPnP/DLNA media server for streaming local media on your home network.
Container.Description: Gerbera is a UPnP media server for serving audio, video, and images to DLNA/UPnP clients such as VLC. This template uses the official gerbera/gerbera Docker image, host networking for multicast discovery, a persistent config/database path at /var/run/gerbera, and a read-only media mount at /mnt/content. The official image automatically scans /mnt/content by default. Do not expose Gerbera directly to the internet; it is intended for trusted LAN or VPN/Tailscale use.

Config.Name: Config / Database
Config.Description: Persistent Gerbera config, database, and runtime files.

Config.Name: Media Folder
Config.Description: Host path containing the media you want Gerbera to serve. This is mounted read-only and is automatically scanned. Example: /mnt/user/media

Config.Name: UID
Config.Description: Container runtime UID. On Unraid, 99 is usually nobody.

Config.Name: GID
Config.Description: Container runtime GID. On Unraid, 100 is usually users.

Config.Name: Web UI / HTTP Port
Config.Description: Gerbera HTTP/Web UI/media-serving port. With host networking, this must remain available on the Unraid host.

Config.Name: SSDP Multicast
Config.Description: UPnP SSDP discovery port. Host networking is recommended because Docker bridge networking does not support the multicast discovery behavior Gerbera needs.
